name: Replace The Failed Interleaved PTX 64x64 Hot-Band Microkernel With The Compact Row-Pair Path

summary.name: Replace The Failed Interleaved PTX 64x64 Hot-Band Microkernel With The Compact Row-Pair Path
summary.hypothesis: The newly added outer-col-step interleave helper at `src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:820-921` failed its own premise. Instead of shrinking the hot-band PTX live set, the measured run landed at 241 registers/thread and the hot-band kernel now dominates the NCU capture. Replacing that helper with the existing compact 64x64 PTX accumulate path (`ptx_wmma_accumulate_tile_set_64x64<false>`) or an equivalently scoped row-pair schedule should recover occupancy and most of the 6.55 ms regression without changing launch geometry, shared layout, or the row-group mapping.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Register-pressure-driven occupancy collapse on the dominant hot-band PTX kernel: 241 registers/thread keeps the kernel at 2 CTAs/SM and leaves tensor utilization low despite unsaturated memory bandwidth.
code_locations: src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:733-922 (generic 64x64 PTX accumulate helper, failed interleaved helper, and dispatch wrapper)
src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:2068-2185 (hot-band PTX store and loop callsite that now routes through the failed helper)
summary.risk: Moderate. The math path stays the same, but removing the interleaved helper can give back some of the intended B-fragment sharing or re-expose prior barrier pressure if the replacement is not kept compact.
ranking_notes: The current dir_01 implementation is directly falsified by measurement: the code comment claims a path back toward the 3-CTA class, but the live run reports 241 registers/thread and a much slower runtime.
The hot-band PTX kernel is the problem surface. `ncu_details_page.csv` shows it consuming 42.27 ms of profiled duration, while the peeled 64x384 and tail kernels are both below 1 ms.
This is the best upside/risk tradeoff because it attacks a fresh localized regression before adding more staging state or reopening a broader family.
summary.stop_condition: Stop if the hot-band PTX kernel still reports more than about 220 registers/thread after the simplification, or if runtime fails to return to the sub-27 ms class while barrier worsens materially.
expected_local_changes: Delete or bypass `ptx_wmma_accumulate_col_step_interleaved_64x64_ptx_microkernel` and stop routing the hot-band PTX kernel through that helper.
Reuse the existing compact 64x64 PTX accumulate helper or a similarly scoped row-pair schedule so A/B fragment lifetimes stay local instead of being spread across the templated outer column loop.
Leave grouped-row mapping, cp.async wide loads, and the current hot-band launch shape unchanged for the first validation pass.

notes: Build passed. Node A must measure this implementation next.
